llm_input.topic: Project 10: The Spy Vault Security Console

meta.title: Spy Vault Security Console
meta.description: Use two slide switches to control a red LED that lights up only when both are toggled on.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding how to use slide switches as input controls for conditional output activation
meta.code_hint: Read both slide switch pins and turn on the LED only when both are HIGH

meta.title: Spy Vault Security Console
meta.description: Use two slide switches to control a red LED that lights up only when both are toggled on.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding how to use slide switches as input controls for conditional output activation
meta.code_hint: Read both slide switch pins and turn on the LED only when both are HIGH

walkthrough.instruction: Place the slide switch on the breadboard with the centre pin in row 12, column E and the side pin in row 13, column E.
walkthrough.tip: Slide it toward the GND side to turn ON — that closes the circuit.

walkthrough.instruction: Place the slide switch on the breadboard with the centre pin in row 17, column E and the side pin in row 18, column E.
walkthrough.tip: Slide it toward the GND side to turn ON — that closes the circuit.

walkthrough.instruction: Put the red LED on the breadboard with the long leg (anode) in row 24, column E and the short leg (cathode) in row 23, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The long leg is positive — it's called the anode!

walkthrough.instruction: Put the 220Ω resistor on the breadboard with one leg in row 23, column D and the other leg in row 19, column D.
walkthrough.tip: The resistor limits current so the component doesn't burn out.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D2 to the breadboard at row 12, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 13, column D to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D3 to the breadboard at row 17, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 18, column D to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D8 to the breadboard at row 24, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 19, column E to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.
